title: Der Tunnel: Roman
summaries: "Der Tunnel: Roman" by Bernhard Kellermann is a novel published in 1913. Engineer Mac Allen rises from coal miner to visionary inventor, dreaming of an audacious feat: a transatlantic tunnel connecting continents beneath the ocean. With industrial backing and global investment, construction begins on a massive scale. But as workers die, disasters strike, and financial scandals erupt, Allen's grand vision threatens to collapse. His personal life crumbles alongside his professional dreams in this tale of technological ambition and human cost. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
